meta.source: trustcare_ui_polished_english_base.json plus screenshot-confirmed UI labels and product redesign synthesis
meta.decision_update: Pharmacy retained in core scope; exhaustive launch scope selected.

core_taxonomy_layers.definition: What is done, including major procedures, therapies, and diagnostic services.

search_modes.description: User enters a symptom, complaint, or intent in plain language. AI maps it to likely specialties, treatments, and facility types without presenting a diagnosis.

search_modes.display_label: Search by Specialty / Treatment / Facility / Provider
search_modes.description: User already knows the doctor, specialty, treatment, procedure, or facility they need.

product_decisions.review_architecture: Provider reviews and facility reviews are separate public trust objects and must never be merged into one public rating.
taxonomy_rules: Treatment / Procedure picker shows treatments, procedures, therapies, and diagnostic services only.
Facility Type picker shows facility types only.
Complementary and alternative care remains searchable but should be visually bucketed and de-emphasized in evidence-first ranking surfaces.
Canonical IDs remain stable even when display labels evolve.

launch_scope.decision: Launch with the full v2 taxonomy set rather than phasing facility types or treatment breadth later.
implications: Provider onboarding, search, and review flows must support pharmacy entities from day one.
Facility reviews must include pharmacy-relevant dimensions where applicable.
Symptom AI mapping and direct search should index the full taxonomy at launch.
Translation coverage and alias coverage must be complete across all launch taxonomies before release.

provider_review_target.display_policy: Show provider rating separately from facility rating.

facility_review_target.display_policy: Show facility rating separately from provider rating.

specialties.notes: Keep; high-value diagnostic and chronic-care specialty.
Add; important for surgery-related discovery and provider taxonomy.
Add; patient-facing specialty separate from broader Genetics & Genomics.
Add; common patient-known surgical intent.
Add; useful for hospital and severity-related matching.
Keep; consumer-facing dual label is important.
Keep, but relate closely to General Practice and Internal Medicine.
Keep; patient-facing label should stay 'General Dentistry'.
Keep, but relate closely to Family Medicine and Internal Medicine.
Add; valuable for aging and mental-health pathways.
Add; common liver-focused search intent.
Keep; important for adult medicine and symptom search.
Add; useful for procedure-led discovery.
Add; important for newborn and NICU-related care.
Keep with full label 'Obstetrics & Gynecology'.
Add; distinct from general oral surgery in patient search.
Keep; distinguish from hands-on rehabilitation service.
Keep as consumer-facing 'Physical Therapy / Physiotherapy'.
Keep; distinct from Psychology / Therapy.
Keep; distinct from Psychiatry.
Keep; high-intent fertility specialty.
Keep as subspecialty-level search target.
Keep, but relate to ENT / Otolaryngology.
Keep; useful bridge between orthopedics and rehab.
Keep; niche but high-intent.

treatment_procedures.notes: Keep searchable, but do not let it dominate primary medical browsing.
Keep searchable, but de-emphasize in evidence-first ranking surfaces.
Keep searchable under a complementary-care bucket.

facility_types.notes: Core scope at launch. Support pharmacy discovery, pharmacy reviews, and pharmacy-specific search aliases.

ui_strings.english_label: Be the first to share an experience and help others.

implementation_priorities: Implement two search modes: Symptom / Concern AI search and Direct taxonomy search.
Launch the full v2 taxonomy at day one: all specialties, treatment / procedure items, and facility types.
Keep Pharmacy in core scope with dedicated entity handling, aliases, and review support.
Maintain separate public review targets and rating models for providers and facilities.
Add robust alias graphs and localized display labels for all taxonomy nodes before launch.
Validate cross-links: symptom -> specialty -> treatment/procedure -> facility type.
Keep complementary and alternative care searchable, but clearly labeled and ranked appropriately.
